# Refresh UX Checklist: Update Grayline Terminator

**Purpose**: Validate that the grayline refresh and user-experience requirements are complete, clear, measurable, and internally consistent before implementation.
**Created**: 2026-05-18
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are all grayline refresh modes documented, including initial display, visible-session refresh, hidden/backgrounded return, device sleep, and update failure? [Completeness, Spec §User Stories, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK002 Are requirements defined for preserving every relevant map state during refresh, including map position, zoom, base map, callsign, WSPR data, selected path, and open controls? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005]
- [x] CHK003 Are requirements complete for long-running sessions beyond the 30-minute success criterion, including multi-hour and overnight behavior? [Coverage, Spec §US1, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK004 Does the spec define which user-facing elements must remain readable and usable while the overlay is present or refreshed? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK005 Are requirements for failed grayline calculations or refreshes documented as recoverable rather than terminal? [Coverage, Spec §FR-007]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is "immediately" for visibility-return refresh defined with enough precision to avoid competing interpretations? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-002a, Spec §SC-005]
- [x] CHK007 Is "subtle" grayline styling clarified with objective criteria or bounded by readability requirements? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK008 Is "active open-map session" defined clearly enough to distinguish visible, hidden, backgrounded, and resumed sessions? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002]
- [x] CHK009 Is "visibly advanced" defined with an objective observation window or comparison method for multi-hour sessions? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002]
- [x] CHK010 Are "current time" and "device time" used consistently as the canonical source for refresh calculations? [Consistency, Spec §FR-003, Spec §Assumptions]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK011 Do the 5-minute cadence requirements align across functional requirements, success criteria, assumptions, and UI contract language? [Consistency, Spec §FR-002, Spec §SC-001]
- [x] CHK012 Are the hidden/backgrounded behavior requirements consistent between the clarification answer, edge cases, functional requirements, and success criteria? [Consistency, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §FR-002a, Spec §SC-005]
- [x] CHK013 Does the no-new-controls scope boundary align with all user stories, assumptions, and plan constraints? [Consistency, Spec §FR-010, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK014 Are failure-handling requirements consistent with the stated goal that core map use must remain uninterrupted? [Consistency, Spec §US2, Spec §FR-007]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK015 Can each success criterion be objectively assessed without relying on implementation details or subjective judgment? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §Success Criteria]
- [x] CHK016 Are the 30-minute and 2-hour measurable outcomes sufficient to validate the user's intended multi-hour grayline movement requirement? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001, Spec §SC-002]
- [x] CHK017 Does the spec define acceptance expectations for both scheduled refresh and visibility-return refresh independently? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §US1, Spec §US3]
- [x] CHK018 Are state-preservation acceptance criteria specific enough to support unambiguous review of map state requirements? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-003]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK019 Are primary, alternate, exception, recovery, and non-functional scenarios represented in the user stories or edge cases? [Coverage, Spec §User Stories, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK020 Are requirements defined for user interaction during the exact moment of a grayline refresh? [Coverage, Spec §US2, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK021 Are requirements for hidden/backgrounded sessions separated from requirements for visible long-running sessions? [Coverage, Spec §US1, Spec §US3]
- [x] CHK022 Are low-power or constrained-device expectations defined with enough specificity to guide requirements review? [Ambiguity, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK023 Are date-line and high-latitude geometry edge cases included in the active grayline refresh requirements, not only in implementation tasks? [Gap, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK024 Are requirements defined for repeated refresh failures or only a single failed update? [Gap, Spec §FR-007]
- [x] CHK025 Are requirements clear for what happens when the device clock changes significantly forward or backward? [Edge Case, Spec §US3]
- [x] CHK026 Are requirements defined for the initial overlay state before the first refresh completes? [Gap, Spec §US1]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK027 Are performance and responsiveness requirements quantified for refresh completion beyond "without visible interruption"? [Clarity, Spec §SC-004]
- [x] CHK028 Are accessibility expectations documented for ensuring the overlay does not reduce readability or operability of controls? [Gap, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK029 Are privacy or analytics boundaries documented for grayline refresh events, especially whether refreshes must avoid custom analytics tracking? [Gap]
- [x] CHK030 Are resource-use expectations clear enough to validate the tradeoff between 5-minute freshness and battery/performance impact? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK031 Are assumptions about device time accuracy explicitly acknowledged as a product requirement constraint? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK032 Are dependencies on existing map state, WSPR data, support links, analytics behavior, and version status traceable to preservation requirements? [Dependency, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK033 Is the no-new-production-dependency assumption reflected as a requirement or clearly scoped to planning only? [Assumption, Plan §Technical Context]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK034 Is there any conflict between conserving resources while hidden and requiring the grayline to remain current during long-open sessions? [Conflict, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-002a]
- [x] CHK035 Are terms normalized across the spec, including "grayline", "terminator", "overlay", and "day/night boundary"? [Terminology, Spec §Key Entities]
- [x] CHK036 Does the spec clearly distinguish requirements for the overlay's calculated accuracy from requirements for its visual presentation? [Clarity, Spec §Key Entities, Spec §SC-002]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
